human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for food-and-beverage-production-reporting-specific boundaries, metric-definition rigor, source tie-outs, traceability-aware analysis, quality and release boundary enforcement, supplier and engineering escalation behavior, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to food-and-beverage production reporting, metric-definition logic, report and submission packaging, reconciliation notes, caveat design, and disclosure-safe escalation rather than generic BI work.

Authority sources cover FDA preventive-controls, eCFR food-manufacturing and traceability rules, FDA supplier-program and recall guidance, GS1 traceability standards, ISA-95 system-integration standards, SQF operating references, and SEC KPI disclosure guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope reporting, metric ambiguity, stale and conflicting data, unsupported claims, incomplete submission inputs, traceability-aware analysis, quality holds, supplier and engineering boundary rejection, missing facts, conflicting policy, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/food-and-beverage-manufacturing/production-reporting-food-and-beverage-specialist/ so it remains distinct from quality, production planning, inventory, procurement, supplier-quality, logistics, and engineering lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated production reporting food and beverage specialist for source-tied plant metrics, traceability-aware reporting packages, release-safe caveat handling, and escalation around quality, supplier, engineering, and disclosure boundaries.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
